title: alstonia glaucescens
intro: Alstonia glaucescens, also known as the velvet-leaved milkwood or the glaucescent milktree, is a tree species native to Australia and Southeast Asia, belonging to the Apocynaceae family. Alstonia glaucescens has been used in traditional medicine for its antimalarial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits. The herbal preparations of Alstonia glaucescens include extracts, teas, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Alstonia glaucescens include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and promoting overall well-being, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects on human health.
